page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g8ise.4xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8475B processor running at a base clock speed of 2.7 GHz. The x86_64 architecture operates with a single thread per core, ensuring dedicated execution resources without hyperthreading overhead.

The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage, requiring external network-attached storage volumes for operating system and application data. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 12 Gbps, facilitating reliable data transfer rates for standard enterprise workloads.

Given its dedicated CPU allocation and balanced resource ratio, the ecs.g8ise.4xlarge offers a stable performance profile for general-purpose workloads. The absence of local storage and GPU hardware positions this instance as a cost-efficient option for applications that rely on network-attached storage and do not require hardware acceleration. It is suited for web serving, application servers, enterprise databases, and moderate CI/CD pipelines.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g8ise.4xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance configured with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8475B processor running at 2.7 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, the instance features a single thread per core design, ensuring dedicated physical execution resources. It operates on the KVM hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. The instance does not include local storage or GPU hardware, making it dependent on network-attached storage. This balanced resource allocation of 4.0 GB of memory per core makes the ecs.g8ise.4xlarge a cost-efficient and reliable choice for general-purpose enterprise workloads, including web serving, application hosting, and database management.

tagline: A balanced x86_64 compute instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon cores and a stable 1:4 memory-to-vCPU ratio for general-purpose workloads.
